Name: Max Knobbout
University: My PhD research was done in the Intelligent Systems group at Utrecht University on the topic of modelling and verifying Normative Multi-Agent Systems. Normative Multi-Agent Systems are large (electronic) systems, like a highway transportation system or an electronic marketplace, consisting of several actors and several norms to regulate the behaviour of these actors. Verification is the act of rigorously proving (or disproving) that the system works as intended. Some of the methods I use are Model Checking and Mathematical/Logical Proof Systems.

Name: Dennis Bakhuis
Field_of_Study: Master Thesis: Effects of boundary variation on Rayleigh-Bénard Convection. Convection is omnipresent, as it can be seen in industrial plants, the oceans, the mantle of our earth and even in the biggest stars. Features of such a system are the ejecting plumes which feed the large scale circulation. In this research we look into the effects of different inhomogeneities on these plumes and their effect on the bulk flow.
